_: uetkx-formatter-cases.json — golden corpus for the .uetkx formatter (D-22 contract mechanism). Run by BOTH the C++ automation suite (ReactiveUI.Uetkx.Formatter) and the lsp-server TS port's node --test (Phase 5); byte-identical output required. `fellBack: true` cases must return the input VERBATIM. Every non-fellback case is also an idempotence pin: format(expect) == expect. Default options: printWidth 100, indentStyle tab, singleAttributePerLine false, insertSpaceBeforeSelfClose true.

cases.name: already canonical is untouched
space indentation normalizes to tabs
setup re-anchors depth-based; authored blank line before return kept
attributes wrap one-per-line past printWidth
include-only preamble canonicalizes to one include per line + one blank
preamble with a comment is preserved byte-for-byte (never delete user content)
@if/@elif/@else: keyword spacing, joined braces, exploded returns
@match: cases and default at one level in, exploded returns
parse error returns the source verbatim with fellBack
trailing content after the component body falls back verbatim (one component per file)
mixed-decl: two components format (one component per file is a LINT convention, not an error)
anonymous fragment
named Fragment keeps the author's spelling and attrs
directive body: lead statements keep whole-body geometry before the return
space-indent option emits spaces
markup comments verbatim; expr nodes get canonical brace spacing
support files (hook/module) round-trip verbatim without fallback
M11: import + export component is already canonical (idempotent)
M11: preamble canonicalizes to #include block, blank, import block, blank
hook: header whitespace + params canonicalize; body re-anchored to tabs
non-exported void hook keeps the arrow omitted
module: verbatim C++ body re-anchored, name canonical
mixed file in source order: module, hook, component (one blank line between)
TB-4: single short text child stays inline; an over-width one still explodes
S4/markup-everywhere: value markup in setup is setup code — reanchored, never restructured (family parity)
S5/es-modules: new-form component is form-preserved (never migrated back to a wrapper)
S5/es-modules: new-form params canonicalize spacing (Type Name = Default)
S5/es-modules: new-form hook keeps its leading return type (no -> migration)
S5/es-modules: value export is one canonical line
cases.input: export FLinearColor Cool = FLinearColor(0.2f, 0.6f, 0.9f, 1.0f);
cases.expect: export FLinearColor Cool = FLinearColor(0.2f, 0.6f, 0.9f, 1.0f);

cases.name: S5/es-modules: messy value spacing canonicalizes
cases.input: export   FLinearColor   Cool =   FLinearColor(0.2f, 0.6f, 0.9f, 1.0f)  ;
cases.expect: export FLinearColor Cool = FLinearColor(0.2f, 0.6f, 0.9f, 1.0f);

cases.name: S5/es-modules: util function form-preserved
S5/es-modules: mixed legacy + new declarations each keep their own form (G-10)
S5/es-modules: rename + namespace + default import heads canonical in the preamble
S5/es-modules: trailing  statement preserved in place
S5/es-modules: a list-exported decl keeps its BARE head (no inline export duplication)
cases.input:
FLinearColor Cool = FLinearColor(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f);
export { Cool };
cases.expect:
FLinearColor Cool = FLinearColor(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f);
export { Cool };

cases.name: S5/es-modules: export list BETWEEN declarations preserved in place
ES combined: default + named import head is canonical (both parts survive the reprint)
ES combined: default + star import head is canonical (both parts survive the reprint)
ES combined: loose spacing in a combined head canonicalizes without dropping a part
